variant: put CeraVe and La Roche-Posay moisturizers side by side in a table

request.message: put CeraVe and La Roche-Posay moisturizers side by side in a table

response_body.response: don't see specific product breakdowns for CeraVe vs La Roche-Posay in your current docs. here's a general comparison based on what those brands typically offer:  both brands lean on ceramides + niacinamide, which matches your protocol's barrier-repair goal. CeraVe runs cheaper and more accessible; La Roche-Posay's thermal water + madecassoside (in cicaplast) is better if you're dealing with active inflammation or rosacea. pick based on your current skin state and budget.

products.name: La Roche-Posay Toleriane Double Repair Face Moisturizer
products.description: Niacinamide + ceramide + glycerin daily moisturizer — derm-recommended.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

products.name: La Roche-Posay Cicaplast Baume B5
products.description: Healing balm with panthenol + madecassoside for compromised barrier.

products.name: CeraVe Moisturizing Cream (Tub)
products.description: Heavy occlusive cream — good for very dry / eczema-prone skin.

products.name: Beauty of Joseon Glow Serum (Propolis + Niacinamide)

rows: daily PM use, all skin types
